content: Docker multi-stage builds: use builder stage for dependencies, copy only artifacts to slim runtime image. Reduces image from ~1GB to <150MB. Pattern: FROM python:3.12-slim AS builder → pip install → FROM python:3.12-slim → COPY --from=builder.

Docker health checks: HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=60s CMD curl -f http://localhost:9000/api/health || exit 1. Start-period gives app time to initialize before checks begin. Compose depends_on respects health status.

GitHub Actions CI pipeline: 3 stages — Quality Gate (lint+test on every push), Build & Push (docker image on main), Deploy (blue-green on tag). Use matrix strategy for Python 3.11+3.12. Cache pip deps with actions/cache for 2-3x faster runs.

Pre-commit hooks with ruff (linting+formatting in one tool, 10-100x faster than flake8+black) and mypy (type checking). Config in .pre-commit-config.yaml. Run on every commit to catch issues before CI. pip install pre-commit && pre-commit install.

Blue-green deployment: maintain two identical environments (blue=current, green=new). Deploy to green, run health checks, swap traffic. If green fails, instant rollback to blue. Requires 2x infrastructure but provides zero-downtime deploys.

Prometheus metrics types: Counter (monotonically increasing, e.g., requests_total), Gauge (can go up/down, e.g., active_connections), Histogram (distribution, e.g., request_duration_seconds with buckets). Use labels for dimensions: {agent='astra', status='success'}.

OpenTelemetry auto-instrumentation for FastAPI: pip install opentelemetry-instrumentation-fastapi. Traces every request automatically. Add opentelemetry-instrumentation-httpx to trace outbound LLM calls. Export to Grafana Tempo, Jaeger, or Langfuse.

Sentry error tracking: free tier covers 5K errors/month. Python SDK auto-captures exceptions, stack traces, breadcrumbs. pip install sentry-sdk[fastapi]. Initialize with sentry_sdk.init(dsn='...', traces_sample_rate=0.1).

Langfuse (self-hosted, MIT license): LLM observability with tracing, cost tracking, prompt management, and evaluation. Docker Compose deployment. Integrates with Anthropic and OpenAI via decorators. Best fit for ROOT's multi-LLM architecture.

PostgreSQL pgvector extension: CREATE EXTENSION vector; then add column: embedding vector(1536). Index with ivfflat or hnsw. Query: SELECT * FROM memories ORDER BY embedding <=> query_embedding LIMIT 10. Zero new infrastructure when migrating from SQLite.

TimescaleDB: PostgreSQL extension for time-series. Hypertables auto-partition by time. Continuous aggregates for pre-computed rollups. Perfect for trading data: prices, signals, portfolio snapshots. CREATE EXTENSION timescaledb; SELECT create_hypertable('prices', 'time');

PostgreSQL connection pooling with PgBouncer: sits between app and database, reuses connections. Pool modes: session (safest), transaction (best performance), statement (most aggressive). Typical pool_size=10, max_overflow=20 for asyncpg.

Redis caching strategies: cache-aside (app checks cache, falls back to DB, writes result to cache), write-through (write to cache and DB simultaneously), write-behind (write to cache, async write to DB). TTL strategy: hot data 5min, warm 1hr, cold 24hr.

Redis Streams for message queues: XADD to publish, XREADGROUP for consumer groups with acknowledgment. Persistent (survives restart), supports exactly-once delivery with XACK. Consumer groups enable load-balanced processing across workers.

Redis semantic cache for LLM responses: store responses indexed by embedding similarity. Use RedisVL's SemanticCache with cosine threshold > 0.90. Cache hits return in milliseconds vs seconds for inference. Achieves up to 73% cost reduction.

SQLite production PRAGMAs: journal_mode=WAL (concurrent reads during writes), synchronous=NORMAL (safe in WAL), cache_size=-64000 (64MB page cache), mmap_size=30000000000 (memory-mapped I/O), temp_store=memory. Run PRAGMA optimize before closing.

SQLite busy_timeout: PRAGMA busy_timeout=5000 (5 seconds). Without this, concurrent writers get immediate SQLITE_BUSY errors. With WAL mode + busy_timeout, SQLite handles reasonable concurrent write loads for single-server deployments.

Litestream: streaming SQLite WAL replication to S3/R2 every 10 seconds. Zero code changes, runs as sidecar. Point-in-time recovery to any second within retention. Cost: ~$0.15/month on Cloudflare R2. Essential for ROOT's 16+ SQLite databases.

NATS JetStream: single-binary message broker with persistent streams, wildcard subscriptions, at-least-once delivery. Maps directly to ROOT's topic patterns (agent.{id}.task, system.alert). Python: pip install nats-py (fully async). Simpler than Kafka.

NATS vs Kafka vs Redis Streams: NATS = simplest operations (single binary), good for <100K msg/s. Kafka = highest throughput (500K+ msg/s), complex ops (ZooKeeper/KRaft). Redis Streams = if you already have Redis, good enough for moderate loads.

Dramatiq task queue: simpler than Celery, supports Redis and RabbitMQ backends, threading-based (good for I/O-bound LLM calls), built-in retries with exponential backoff, middleware pipeline. 10x faster than Python-RQ. pip install dramatiq[redis].

Structured logging with structlog: JSON output, automatic context binding (request_id, agent_id, trace_id), processor pipeline for enrichment. Replaces Python stdlib logging. Integration with Sentry and OpenTelemetry for correlated traces.

Anthropic prompt caching: cached reads cost 0.1x base input (90% savings). Break-even after 2 cache hits. Structure prompts with stable prefixes (system prompt, tool definitions) before variable content (query, history) to maximize cache hits.

LLM cost optimization combined strategy: model cascading (50-70% savings) + prompt caching (30-50%) + batch API for background tasks (50%) + semantic caching (20-40%) + prompt compression (15-25%). Combined: 60-80% total cost reduction.

Hetzner VPS: 4 vCPU, 8GB RAM for ~$7/month — best price-to-performance for small deployments. Combined with Coolify (self-hosted PaaS, free), Cloudflare R2 ($0.15/mo), Redis (Upstash free tier): total $8-10/month production stack.

Fly.io deployment: persistent volumes for SQLite, global edge deployment, Litestream integration well-documented. fly launch + fly volumes create. Good for apps needing multiple regions. ~$1.94/month base + volume costs.

DuckDB for in-process analytics: reads Parquet, CSV, SQLite files directly. Columnar storage optimized for aggregation queries. Perfect for backtesting analytics without impacting main database. Import: pip install duckdb. Zero server needed.

uvloop replaces Python's default asyncio event loop with Cython/libuv implementation. 2-4x throughput improvement with 1000+ concurrent connections. Enable: uvicorn --loop uvloop (already included in uvicorn[standard]). Verify it's active in production.

Pure ASGI middleware provides 40% better performance than FastAPI's BaseHTTPMiddleware. BaseHTTPMiddleware creates 7 intermediate objects per request. ROOT already uses pure ASGI for security middleware — maintain this pattern for all middleware.

orjson is 2-10x faster than stdlib json for serialization/deserialization. Use FastAPI's ORJSONResponse as default response class. ROOT already includes orjson in requirements. Ensure all JSON operations use orjson where possible.

asyncio.Semaphore(n) limits concurrent access to resources. Starting points: 5-20 for database ops, 10-50 for HTTP calls, 3-5 for LLM API calls (match rate limits). Monitor acquire_time p95 — if >100ms, the limit is too low or downstream is too slow.

aiosqlite for async SQLite: async with aiosqlite.connect(path) as db. Enable WAL: await db.execute('PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL'). Use executemany for batch inserts — 10-100x faster than individual inserts. Single connection per DB with WAL is sufficient.

JWT authentication: short-lived access tokens (15 min) + long-lived refresh tokens (7 days). Store refresh tokens server-side for revocation. Use python-jose for encoding/decoding. FastAPI OAuth2PasswordBearer dependency for route protection.

SOPS + age for secrets management: encrypt .env values in git while keeping keys readable. sops --encrypt --age $(cat age-key.pub) .env > .env.enc. 30 minutes to set up. Better than plain .env files in repos, even private ones.

E2B sandboxing: Firecracker microVM boots in <200ms with isolated kernel, filesystem, network. Sessions up to 24 hours. Python SDK: pip install e2b. Best for ROOT's self-writing code — test proposals in sandbox before deploying.

Container log management: docker-compose logging with json-file driver, max-size 20MB, max-file 5. For production: forward to Loki (Grafana stack) or ELK. Use correlation IDs to trace requests across containers.

Database backup strategy for ROOT: Litestream for streaming WAL replication (10s intervals), SQLite backup() API for periodic full snapshots (daily), filesystem snapshots for atomic multi-DB consistency. Test restore monthly.

Load testing with Locust: Python-native, define user behavior as code, distributed mode for high load. Test WebSocket connections, API endpoints, and concurrent agent operations. Establish baselines before optimization: p95 latency, max RPS, error rate.

HTTP/2 multiplexing: multiple requests over single TCP connection, reducing latency. Header compression (HPACK) reduces overhead. Server push pre-sends resources. Enable in nginx: listen 443 ssl http2. Most LLM APIs support HTTP/2 natively.

TLS best practices: use Let's Encrypt for free auto-renewing certs. HSTS header with max-age=31536000 and includeSubDomains. Disable TLS 1.0/1.1, use TLS 1.3 preferred. Cipher suite: ECDHE-AESGCM-SHA256 minimum.

Alembic database migrations for Python/SQLAlchemy: alembic revision --autogenerate -m 'description' generates migration from model changes. alembic upgrade head applies migrations. alembic downgrade -1 rolls back. Essential for schema versioning.

Connection pooling patterns: create pool at startup (not per request), reuse connections, set pool_size based on expected concurrency. For asyncpg: pool = await asyncpg.create_pool(dsn, min_size=5, max_size=20). Release connections promptly.

Grafana dashboards: connect to Prometheus for metrics, Loki for logs, Tempo for traces. Pre-built FastAPI dashboard available (ID: 16110). Custom panels for ROOT: agent task rates, LLM costs, memory growth, trading P&L.

Rate limiting per endpoint: use sliding window counter in Redis. Key: rate_limit:{user_id}:{endpoint}:{window}. Different limits per endpoint: /api/chat = 30/min, /api/tasks = 100/min, /api/health = unlimited. Return X-RateLimit-* headers.

CORS configuration for production: explicitly list allowed origins (never use *). Allow credentials only when needed. Preflight cache: Access-Control-Max-Age: 86400 (24hrs). Separate CORS policy for API vs static assets.

Content-Security-Policy header: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data:; connect-src 'self' wss: https:;. Prevents XSS by restricting resource loading. Test with report-only mode first.

Kubernetes basics: Pod (smallest unit, 1+ containers), Deployment (manages pod replicas), Service (stable network endpoint), ConfigMap/Secret (configuration), PersistentVolumeClaim (storage). HPA auto-scales based on CPU/custom metrics.

WebSocket scaling: each connection holds state in memory. For horizontal scaling, use Redis pub/sub to broadcast messages across workers. Sticky sessions (nginx ip_hash) keep clients connected to same worker. Monitor connection count per worker.

API versioning strategies: URL path (/v1/users) is simplest and most common. Header-based (Accept-Version: 2) is cleaner but less discoverable. Never break existing API versions — deprecate with sunset headers and migration guides.

Infrastructure as Code: Terraform for cloud resources, Docker Compose for local/single-server, Kubernetes manifests for orchestration. Coolify provides push-to-deploy PaaS on your own server — combines git integration with container management for free.
